governing_requirement: Implement receipt-last atomic commit, restart reconstruction and outcome-unknown reconciliation before retry, decision-time health/capability/revocation evidence, coordinated GPU leases, cache freshness, and installed service/node-pack drift rejection; fault injection at every durable boundary must prove no duplicate execution, orphan promotion, or authority drift.
prerequisites: MF-P6-10.07 has no accepted consumer invalidation/cache/rollback execution.
MF-P6-11.06 has no accepted durable bridge journal or materialized state.
The bounded port-8765 follow-up is locally down after Windows localhost forwarding failed; this does not block the static/focused audit.
verified_foundations: The frozen lifecycle permits receipt_last_atomic_commit only on receipt_committed and has explicit submission_unknown plus four exact reconciliation outcomes; only verified remote not-found may authorize one resubmission.

The signed receipt binds request/idempotency IDs, exact release and capability hashes, admission/queue/start/completion timing, selected route, worker/device/lease identity, observed resources, artifacts, transforms, QA, authority, revocation check, lineage, and eligibility.

Receipt validation recomputes durations, requires the lease to cover completion, validates request/receipt/release/capability/certificate bindings when exact external documents are supplied, and enforces fresh revocation evidence for production use.
The release contract requires byte hashes for a ComfyUI node-pack artifact and rejects unmanifested release files; cache/invalidation/rollback lifecycle vocabulary is signed and closed.
The live runtime uses a token-bound project GPU lock, refuses a live competing owner, reports stale owners without deleting them, and releases only its own unchanged token.
Thirteen focused contract, reconciliation, release-closure, installer, GPU-lock, and runtime-lease tests pass; Ruff passes.

commands: python -m ruff check src/maskfactory/validation.py src/maskfactory/gpu.py src/maskfactory/serve/api.py src/maskfactory/serve/comfy_install.py tests/test_mask_bridge_contracts_v1.py tests/test_gpu.py tests/test_comfy_export.py tests/test_serve_api.py
direct unresolved-receipt commit, capability-drift, and stale-node reinstall probes

direct_probes.setup: A trusted producer receipt_committed event carried a made-up receipt payload digest and correct schema digest, but no receipt document or artifact bytes were supplied.
direct_probes.gap: The in-memory journal validator proves event shape/transition only; it cannot prove the receipt exists, is valid, or was committed after every artifact/state write.

direct_probes.interpretation: A re-signed receipt with a changed capability snapshot hash is internally valid. Drift is detected only when the caller supplies the exact external capability/certificate context; no runtime captures/resolves current decision-time state.

direct_probes.gap: The developer installer copies in place with dirs_exist_ok and cannot prove exact installed bytes, reject stale nodes, switch atomically, or roll back.

direct_probes.observed: No bridge journal/recovery module or receipt/artifact transaction store exists. Existing orchestrator caches, atomic writers, GPU lock, and unrelated DAZ/control stores are not contract-native bridge state.
direct_probes.gap: Restart cannot reconstruct admitted/submitted/result/validated/receipt/cache/adoption state from authoritative bridge records.

confirmed_gaps: No transaction stages artifacts and state, fsyncs them, atomically publishes exact outputs, signs a validated receipt, appends receipt_committed, and advances an authenticated checkpoint last.
No recovery entry point replays a durable journal, finds incomplete attempts, resolves exact remote execution identities, and forbids resubmission until signed/current not-found evidence exists.
No runtime provider API supports exact status/history reconciliation for a submitted-unknown identity or returns result/not-found evidence bound to the frozen reconciliation fields.
No decision transaction captures exact health, capability, adopted release, revocation head/freshness, installed service/OpenAPI, node-pack inventory, route, GPU lease, and policy state together.
No cache store binds request/receipt/artifact/authority/revocation/release/capability/node-pack keys, freshness, tombstone/rebuild events, and exact immutable bytes.
The current node installer mutates a live directory in place, retains stale files, emits no closed manifest/hash receipt, and has no atomic version switch or rollback.
The GPU lock is a safe local mutual-exclusion primitive but is not durably bound to journal lease events, request identity, device/resource observations, or restart reconciliation.

No kill matrix covers reservation, admission, lease, submission known/unknown, provider result, artifact write/fsync/rename, receipt sign/write, event/state/checkpoint, cache publish, install switch, or rollback boundaries.

No stale pack, changed capability, lock contention, service restart, cache corruption, rollback interruption, or concurrent recovery proof establishes no duplicate execution/orphan promotion/authority drift.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement additive producer receipt-last transaction and recovery conformance over the accepted durable journal: exact staged artifact/receipt/checkpoint ordering, unknown-outcome reconciliation, decision-time state capture, lease/cache/drift evidence, and crash-safe recovery without implementing Main-owned cache/install/DAG actions.
implementation_packet.path_state: All five allowed paths are absent and clean. Candidate paths remain read-only. Scope is disjoint from frozen v1 contracts, preserved MF-P6-08.05 dirty paths, and prepared MF-P6-10.07/11.06/11.07 paths.
validator_commands: python -m pytest -q tests/test_bridge_recovery.py tests/test_mask_bridge_contracts_v1.py tests/test_gpu.py tests/test_comfy_export.py -k 'receipt or unknown or reconciliation or cache or lease or install or rollback or recovery'
python -m ruff check src/maskfactory/bridge/recovery.py tools/recover_bridge_transactions.py tests/test_bridge_recovery.py
acceptance_criteria: Use the accepted durable journal/idempotency engine; do not create a second authority store. Persist exact transaction phase, request/body, remote identity, staged artifacts, receipt, lease, snapshots, cache decision, and checkpoint references.

Publish validated artifacts first, then the signed receipt and receipt_committed event, with the authenticated state/checkpoint last. A receipt event must resolve and validate exact receipt/artifact bytes before state gains authority.

On restart, replay authoritative records and reconcile every submitted/outcome-unknown attempt through exact provider history identity. Only verified current not-found evidence permits one bounded resubmission.

Capture and hash exact health, capability, adopted release, revocation, service/OpenAPI, installed node-pack, policy, route, and GPU lease observations at decision time; stale, missing, contradictory, or changed context fails closed.

Bind producer cache evidence to exact request/receipt/artifact/source/owner/transform/authority/certificate/revocation/release/capability/node-pack identities and freshness. Validate signed Main tombstone/rebuild evidence without mutating Main cache state.

Validate exact installed node-pack inventory/manifest and atomic version/rollback evidence from Main; reject stale/unmanifested/missing/changed nodes and never treat the developer copy installer as production adoption.

Coordinate the local GPU token lease with exact request/attempt/device/resource and journal evidence; contention/refusal/loss/stale-owner cases are typed, and cleanup never deletes a replacement owner's lock.

Kill/fault tests at every durable boundary plus stale pack, capability/revocation drift, lock contention, cache corruption, service restart, and rollback prove deterministic recovery, no duplicate execution, no orphan promotion, and no authority drift.
Frozen v1 contracts, preserved dirty paths, disabled scheduled maintenance, worker records, and unrelated WSL/Docker/CVAT state remain unchanged.

claim_boundary: This audit credits the strict receipt/event/release contracts, unknown-submission decision matrix, receipt timing/lease/revocation checks, release-byte closure, GPU-lock safety, and existing atomic primitives only. It does not implement receipt-last persistence, restart reconstruction, live remote reconciliation, decision-time snapshot capture, cache freshness, exact installed node-pack drift checks, rollback, or the required crash/fault matrix, and it does not complete MF-P6-11.08.
